title: Displacement
short: The straight-line change in position, from start to finish, measured in metres (m).
full:
Displacement is the vector from an object's starting point to its ending point. It differs from distance travelled: a runner who completes one lap of a track covers a large distance but has zero displacement, because they end where they began.

In the definition of work, W = F·s, the s is the displacement along the direction of the force.

The SI unit is the metre (m).
